title: Warm Buttery Egyptian Lentil Casserole
description: A buttery and cheesy Egyptian dish featuring almond flour and a mirepoix base, perfect for a crowd, and served in a mason jar. This shojin-friendly recipe is a delight to eat and fun to make.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, saute the chopped onion, carrot, and celery in butter until tender.
Add the lentils, water, salt, and pepper to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
In a small bowl, mix the almond flour and nutritional yeast.
Stir the almond flour mixture into the lentil mixture and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Transfer the lentil mixture to a mason jar and top with additional butter if desired.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 20 minutes or until the top is golden brown.
